title: Unsupported DBMS Storage Formats
text: Unsupported DBMS Storage Formats It is possible that your DBMS supports a storage format that is not covered by a Uniface connector-level packing code. Depending on the extent of the incompatibility, this can be solved by: Recognizing this limitation and never using that DBMS storage format. This is the only solution if Uniface has no data type that corresponds to the storage format in the DBMS, and no packing code that provides the data in a format that you could convert to match the DBMS storage format. This can be the case when the DBMS storage format is so exotic that there is no remotely similar Uniface packing code. By not supporting a DBMS storage format in your connector, you do not limit the Uniface functionality. However, the connector will not be able to access fields in the database with an unsupported storage format. Carrying out the required data conversion in the connector itself. If the unsupported DBMS storage format is similar to one of the Uniface packing codes, you can choose that packing code and make sure that the connector handles the required format conversions in the data manipulation connector requests. CAUTION: This is an extremely complicated approach; there are no connector service functions available to help you carry out the conversion, so be warned that this solution is your responsibility. Related Links Database Connector Interface The Database Connector Interface is an API for building custom database connectors. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
